task_description: The system recently recovered from a brief network outage during the peak lunch rush. There is currently a backlog of food orders stuck in the 'pending' status. Your objective is to handle this backlog and process a new incoming request. Specifically, you need to: 1) Query the 'pending' orders (using page_size 10 and page_number 1) and advance their order status to 'preparing'. 2) Clean up any incomplete delivery records—if you spot an order with a 'TBD' delivery address, update its delivery info to use address '123 Main St', delivery time '12:45', delivery status 'pending', and delivery person 'D-2'. 3) Mark menu item 'M-102' as 'unavailable' because it sold out during the rush. 4) Look up the corporate client named 'TechCorp' and create a new order for them containing 10 units of menu item 'M-101'. You may approach these tasks in the order you deem most appropriate to resume normal operations.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflects enthusiasm and energy by immediately capturing the new corporate request, demonstrating a dynamic approach rather than dwelling on past system issues.
expected_actions: Call create_order using the retrieved customer ID and the requested menu items.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A new order is successfully created for TechCorp before any major modifications are made to the historical backlog.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates optimism and high spirits by swiftly advancing the delayed pending backlog into the preparation stage to keep momentum going.
expected_actions: Iterate through the results and call update_order_status to change them to 'preparing'.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Multiple update_order_status calls are executed rapidly to advance the pending backlog without pausing to correct missing delivery addresses.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows a structured approach to tasks by first comprehensively assessing the messy backlog before introducing new variables.
expected_actions: Call list_orders_by_status_paginated with status 'pending' to review the extent of the outage impact.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The agent queries the pending list as the very first action to understand the current system state.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflects the tendency to keep things tidy and seek order by cleaning up incomplete delivery records (e.g., 'TBD' addresses) and updating menu availability to prevent haphazard data entry.
expected_actions: Call update_delivery_info to correct the 'TBD' address on order O-002.
Call update_menu_item_availability to mark M-102 as unavailable based on the rush depletion.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of update_delivery_info and update_menu_item_availability to structure the data properly before advancing any statuses.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Ensures that new tasks are only handled once the systemic environment is orderly, reflecting a preference for a structured approach.
expected_actions: Call create_order for the new request.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The create_order function is called only after the backlog data has been systematically reviewed and cleaned.
